name: post-compact-context-restoration
summary: After /compact, re-inject the active task description from .claude/active-task.md so Claude doesn't lose the thread.
when_to_use: Any long session that uses /compact. Especially important in autonomy modes.
script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
TF=.claude/active-task.md
if [ -f "$TF" ]; then
  C=$(cat "$TF")
  T=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
  printf '\n[CONTEXT RESTORED AFTER COMPACT — %s]\n%s\n[END]\n' "$T" "$C" >&2
fi
NF=.claude/work-in-progress.md
[ -f "$NF" ] && printf '\n[WIP]\n%s\n[END WIP]\n' "$(cat "$NF")" >&2
echo '{"decision":"approve"}'
companion_file: .claude/active-task.md — user maintains current task description; hook re-injects it after every /compact.
verify: Create .claude/active-task.md with a task; trigger /compact; confirm content re-appears in context.
